introSection: Merlin's Mayhem is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Dutch Wonderland in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, USA. Opened in 2018, it is the first "Family Inverted Coaster" from S&S Worldwide. The ride passes over Sky Ride.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The cars were revealed at S&S's booth at the 2016 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. In May 2017, the ride went vertical. The steel was fabricated by OCEM Structural Steelwork.

The attraction was originally scheduled to open in 2017, but was delayed to 2018 due to the final touches with the ride's theming and station being pushed back. It opened on April 28.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content:
The story behind the ride, which is revealed in the queue line, features magician Merlin on an adventure to find his dragon friend, Mayhem. R&R Creative Amusement Designs was contracted to provide thematic design for all the buildings and sets, graphic signage, audio and lighting systems, script writing, and media production.

The ride features onboard sound.

fullText:
Merlin's Mayhem is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Dutch Wonderland in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, USA. Opened in 2018, it is the first "Family Inverted Coaster" from S&S Worldwide. The ride passes over Sky Ride.

The cars were revealed at S&S's booth at the 2016 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. In May 2017, the ride went vertical. The steel was fabricated by OCEM Structural Steelwork.

The attraction was originally scheduled to open in 2017, but was delayed to 2018 due to the final touches with the ride's theming and station being pushed back. It opened on April 28.

The story behind the ride, which is revealed in the queue line, features magician Merlin on an adventure to find his dragon friend, Mayhem. R&R Creative Amusement Designs was contracted to provide thematic design for all the buildings and sets, graphic signage, audio and lighting systems, script writing, and media production.

The ride features onboard sound.
